item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is actually something that I've been meaning to get to when it was pointed out to me a year ago. Just gonna… fix that in a few minutes. And fixed. Should be more fitting now.

Thank you for the spot and the read!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is an idea that I inherently like, and I enjoyed the execution. I’m confused about this, though:

To date, all figurines produced from SCP-3754-O instances are unique and possess an identifying tag.

If they’re all unique, how can some be more common or rare?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, alright, you get my +1 because I literally gasped out loud at the last section. I hope you're happy!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The tone is spot-on! Some awkward wordings here and there, but nothing terrible.

The user reviews look and sound like real user reviews, I enjoyed that. I don't think "influence on its community" goes far enough, you need to spell it out a little more: Something's wrong with these people. For me to buy this, you need to comment on it further.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hi! Thank you for reading and your input.

This is the third time I've gotten a comment on this specific thing. My reasoning is that though it can eat physical matter, it isn't always successful in doing so. It makes an attempt, and if it succeeds, it consumes. I didn't think that it would be that big of a deal.

However, since I've gotten repeated comments on this singular bit, I went ahead and removed "attempt to". Hopefully that alleviates the issue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a really off putting article in the best way. I think that the formatting adds a lot to the reading and you can tell that a lot of work went into the reviews alone. I am a little confused because you jump from saying that SCP-3754 would attempt to eat physical matter, to it actually doing so.

…SCP-3754 will typically wait until contact has been made with the screen and attempt to consume the physical matter. This is followed by simulated sound bytes of chewing and gulping, occasionally accompanied by a low-frequency vibration from the host device.

Jumps to:

This led to players starting rumors that SCP-3754's consumption of human flesh increased the possibility of an SCP-3754-O instance yielding a rare mini-figurine.

This is my only real issue with an otherwise extremely well written and eerie article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All the reviewers seem to be rather nonchalant about the fact that you can extract physical items out of the screen and it eats your fingers. I would like to know what kind of games they're playing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is a good concept and it worked in creeping me out, but when you brought this up in declassified last night I was under the impression you would fix some of the things I pointed out.

This still feels so unrealistic to me. The thing is eating people (the public media must be aware of this to some degree) and yet this gets to stay up for nearly two months because it has a fan base? I'm aware that 'there is no canon' but that is not to mention that this thing was created by a GoI the foundation must be aware of as well.

Unless I missed something, this anomaly from an outsider perspective still doesn't make logical sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the second skip I’ve seen referencing “Accelerate the Future”. I don’t know if they’ve been a prominent group, but I can say that, out of all the groups of interest I’m well-versed in the knowledge of, Accelerate the Future is among the most thoroughly fucked up.

Anyway, good shit is good. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for pointing that out. I implemented a small fix to that particular section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If SCP-3754 is given an item purchased from the in-app store and then fed a free item, the entity will respond with hostility and a desire to be fed exclusively with items from the in-app store.

I don't understand this line, and part about the organic consumption wasn't mentioned until last paragraph of description, so it comes out as sudden—unless I miss something? It felt a bit tacky, like it's graphic only for the sake of being graphic.

Withholding my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this when I critted it, and I like it even more now!! The CSS is amazing — go Uncle Nico!! +1 from me <3

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Free to play, but at what cost?

Big thank you to keyii, OthellotheCat, magnadeus, and Hot-coles for their critique!
Big thank you to Uncle Nicolini for not just critique, but that super nice review CSS!
This article is dedicated to Dog Teeth for their creation of ATF, a horrible business for horrible people. You can find more ATF in articles like SCP-3312 and SCP-4599!
Most of all, thank you for reading!

Like this article and want to see more of what I've written on the site? Check out SCP-3757!

Image Attributions
In order of appearance

tiffy_tee00 = Nico's dog when she was a puppy (her name is Panda Bear). Thanks, Nico.
Lorraine = https://flic.kr/p/2a1HuiQ
Sammy = My friend/roommate Jess, taken by me. Thanks, Jess.
Stan = Me. I took this. like four years ago.
daniel = https://flic.kr/p/2b5wqmZ
Ryan = My friend Justin, taken by Justin. Thanks, Justin.
